name: Hot-Pepper Pumpkin Scones
recipeIngredient: 1 tbsp Tabasco, or other hot sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375 degrees.
2. In a small skillet, melt the butter over medium heat. Add chopped onion and jalapeno and stir until onion is soft (about 2 minutes). Set aside to cool.
3. In a large bowl, mix the flour, baking powder, soda, cinnamon, ginger, allspice, salt, and sugar. Mix in the melted butter until mixture resembles grains of rice.
4. Make a well in the center of the dry ingredients and add buttermilk, pumpkin puree, and egg to the well. Mix together with a fork and gradually work in the mixture until a soft, cohesive dough forms.
5. Drop the batter 1/3 cupful at a time, 2 inches apart on a lightly greased cookie sheet. Bake in a preheated oven for 20 minutes, until puffed and firm on surface, but fluffy in the center.
6. Transfer to a cooling rack and cover loosely with a dish towel. Cool for at least 5 minutes.
7. Mix honey with the jalapeno sauce and brush tops of the scones with this mixture before serving.
